title: I Went To Heaven,
content:
I went to heaven, --
    'T was a small town,
    Lit with a ruby,
    Lathed with down.
    Stiller than the fields
    At the full dew,
    Beautiful as pictures
    No man drew.
    People like the moth,
    Of mechlin, frames,
    Duties of gossamer,
    And eider names.
    Almost contented
    I could be
    'Mong such unique
    Society.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "I Went To Heaven,", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
